research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
case_title: Brian delayed spinal metastatic cancer diagnosis
condition_or_issue: severe back pain after prior breast cancer
initial_situation: Patient had severe back pain and a prior cancer history.
what_happened: Multiple contacts across ED, GPs, out-of-hours GPs, nurse, and physiotherapy preceded later diagnosis.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Prior cancer history plus repeated unresolving back pain were not connected early.
outcome_if_known: Spinal metastatic cancer diagnosed after spinal lump and CT.
lesson_or_pattern: Repeated contacts across fragmented care should trigger a diagnostic review.
product_or_system_implication: Unresolved-symptom timeline and prior high-risk history surfacing.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
case_title: Delayed urgent recognition in online skin-lesion consultation
condition_or_issue: worsening skin lesion with prior skin cancer
initial_situation: Patient submitted online request about skin symptoms.
what_happened: A second online request lacked prior cancer history and was triaged routine; later GP review generated urgent referral.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Worsening skin signs and prior skin cancer history were not joined at intake/triage.
delay_or_timeline: 2 weeks to GP suspicion after second form, plus referral wait
lesson_or_pattern: Online forms need context from records and recent related contacts.
product_or_system_implication: Hybrid intake with prior-history auto-pull and adaptive red flags.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
condition_or_issue: common diseases including pneumonia, CHF, renal failure, cancer, UTI/pyelonephritis
initial_situation: Patients had primary-care encounters preceding later signals of diagnostic error.
what_happened: Researchers used EHR triggers to identify and review diagnostic-error cases.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Breakdowns occurred in history, exam, workup, referral, and tracking.
delay_or_timeline: Trigger examples included hospitalization/return/ED within 14 days
outcome_if_known: Many cases had moderate or severe harm potential.
lesson_or_pattern: Common diseases can be missed when several small process steps fail.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
case_title: Nurse handover communication barriers in Ghanaian referral hospitals
setting_or_context: referral hospitals: ED, ICU, wards, maternity
initial_situation: Patients moved across shifts and units where nurses handed over care.
what_happened: Interviews found poor documentation, lack of formal procedures, and communication barriers.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Diagnosis, treatment plans, medication changes, and pending tasks may be missed.
outcome_if_known: Study linked handover gaps to diagnostic/treatment errors and complications risk.
lesson_or_pattern: Handoff quality is a diagnostic-safety issue, not only an administrative issue.
product_or_system_implication: Structured handoff including uncertainty, pending tests, and next owner.
